A.text: on one side of a backbone, without connecting to the opposite backbone

B.text: only at the start and end of each backbone

C.text: along the outer surface of each backbone

parsed.rationale: The image clearly shows the nitrogen bases (colored blocks) positioned in the center of the double helix structure. They are depicted connecting the two separate sugar-phosphate backbones that form the outer sides of the helix. This arrangement places the bases directly between the two backbones, rather than on the outside or at the ends.
